scopeNote: Recovery-review is clean on the current propagated stack because the re-anchored review evidence now resolves on the current branch, including the latest PR #102 traceability re-check for the three previously stale review-artifact commit anchors, so the restored pass state is auditable rather than a stale clean summary carried forward from pre-restack history.

lint.command: bash ci/lint-knowledgebase.sh && bash ci/check-architecture.sh && bash ci/check-enterprise-policy.sh && bash ci/check-orchestrator-layer.sh && python3 scripts/check_flaky_tags.py --tests-root erp-domain/src/test/java --gate gate-fast && bash scripts/guard_openapi_contract_drift.sh

appliedUpdates.description: Re-anchored the aggregate recovery-review traceability chain to the current propagated commits, replacing the three stale non-ancestor review anchors with reachable propagated commits: final-hardening `373e705819bdebb6e7166b1ce1528eb3cebf1f39`, portal `42d93ea5a3182308d50dddf3db634492f72ffd8c`, and the docs-only PR #102 shareout review `3e1ac865c2c4110d60d25cf50634e63362b9b753`, so the milestone pass now reflects a re-established auditable stack rather than a stale clean summary from before restack churn was closed.

Recorded the aggregate traceability verification result explicitly: all eight linked recovery-review artifacts now point at commitIds that resolve on the current propagated branch, so the reported 8/8 pass count no longer overstates replayable evidence.

rejectedObservations.observation: The scrutiny-feature-reviewer Task droid failed to launch for every recovery-review feature with `Invalid model: custom:CLIProxyAPI-5.4-medium`, so all feature reviews were completed manually from mission handoffs, progress logs, and commit diffs.

The P2P review surfaced a runtime-probe contract mismatch between `.factory/services.yaml` and `testing/ci/test_pr_review_ci_packet.py`, but resolving it would require a non-additive manifest change outside this validator packet and outside the required recovery-review validator surface.
